title: Fantastic Dense Greek Chinese Long Beans Kleftiko
description: A bitter, dense, and indulgent Greek dish featuring Chinese long beans, cooked in a traditional ttukbaegi earthenware vessel and served in a scallop shell. This satvik-friendly recipe is a unique fusion of flavors and textures.

ingredients.name: Chinese long beans
ingredients.notes: trimmed and cut into 2 inch pieces

instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the Chinese long beans with olive oil, onion, garlic, lemon juice, oregano, thyme, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Transfer the bean mixture to a ttukbaegi earthenware vessel or a scallop shell.
Cover the vessel with aluminum foil and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 20 minutes, or until the beans are tender and caramelized.
Remove the vessel from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Serve the kleftiko hot, garnished with additional lemon wedges and thyme if desired.
Traditionally, kleftiko is cooked in a pit or a wood fired oven, but this recipe adapts it for home cooking.
You can also cook the kleftiko in a slow cooker on low for 2-3 hours.
